example.gloss: I'm wearing a white T-shirt.
This shirt is too big.

words.note: pluralia tantum — always plural, like English 'pants'

example.gloss: Where are my trousers?
Today I'm wearing jeans.
She bought a new dress.
That skirt is very pretty.
In winter I wear a warm coat.
Take a jacket, it's cold outside.
This sweater is made of wool.
I can't find the other sock.
These shoes are too small for me.
I need new sneakers for running.
In winter I wear boots.
At home I wear slippers.
In summer I wear a hat.
Put on a cap, it's cold!
I wear a scarf around my neck.
I lost one glove.
I need a new belt for my trousers.

words.note: the word comes from 'Hrvat' — the necktie is a Croatian invention

example.target: Na poslu nosi košulju i kravatu.
example.gloss: At work he wears a shirt and a tie.
I can't see well without glasses.
She rarely wears jewelry.
This ring is made of gold.
She got a necklace as a gift.
My wallet is in my handbag.
It's raining, take an umbrella!

example.target: Imate li ovo u većoj veličini?
example.gloss: Do you have this in a bigger size?
What is your favorite color?
Snow is white.
I have a black cat.
The tomato is red.

words.note: for hair it means blond: plava kosa

example.gloss: The sky is blue today.
Grass is green.
The lemon is yellow.
She has brown eyes.

example.target: Nebo je sivo kad pada kiša.
example.gloss: The sky is gray when it rains.
She bought a pink dress.
The flower is purple.
The orange is orange.
He's wearing a colorful T-shirt.
I like light colors.
She's wearing a dark jacket.

words.note: one verb for both meanings — context decides

example.gloss: What are you wearing today?

words.note: pf. odjenuti se; everyday synonym: oblačiti se

example.gloss: I get dressed quickly in the morning.
Put on a warm jacket!
Can I try on this dress?
That dress suits you well.
Our building has five floors.
I live on the third floor.
The elevator isn't working, we're going on foot.
I take the stairs to the second floor.
The TV is in the living room.

example.target: Spavaća soba je mala, ali svijetla.
example.gloss: The bedroom is small but bright.
We have lunch in the dining room.
We leave our shoes in the hallway.
You can see the sea from the balcony.
The children are playing in the yard.

example.target: Baka ima lijep vrt iza kuće.
example.gloss: Grandma has a nice garden behind the house.
The car is in the garage.
We keep old things in the basement.
The cat is on the roof.
A picture hangs on the wall.

words.note: also a preposition: pod stolom = under the table

example.gloss: The floor is made of wood.
The lamp hangs from the ceiling.

example.target: Kupili smo novi namještaj za stan.
example.gloss: We bought new furniture for the apartment.

example.target: Sjedimo na kauču i gledamo film.
example.gloss: We're sitting on the couch watching a movie.
Grandpa reads the newspaper in the armchair.
The clothes are in the wardrobe.
The books are on the shelf.
There's a big rug on the floor.
The curtains are white.
Turn on the lamp, it's dark.
I'm looking at myself in the mirror.
This picture is very old.
The milk is in the fridge.
The soup is on the stove.
The cake is in the oven.

words.note: perilica rublja = washing machine; perilica posuđa = dishwasher; colloquially: veš-mašina

example.gloss: The washing machine is in the bathroom.
The dishes are in the sink.
Take a clean towel from the wardrobe.
I sleep on two pillows.
In winter I need a warm blanket.
I change the bed linen every week.
Wash your hands with soap.
I pay rent every month.
Our neighbor is very kind.
What is your address?

words.note: stanujem; where you live, vs živjeti = to live in general

example.target: Stanujem u malom stanu u centru.
example.gloss: I live in a small apartment in the center.

words.note: impf. iznajmljivati; used for both renting and renting out

example.gloss: We want to rent an apartment in Zagreb.

example.target: Preselili smo se u novu kuću.
example.gloss: We moved into a new house.
I have to tidy my room.
On Saturdays I clean the house.
Put the plates on the table.
A glass of water, please.
I drink a cup of coffee every morning.
I eat soup with a spoon.
I need a fork and a knife.
This knife cuts well.
The salad is in a big bowl.
The soup is cooking in the pot.
I'm frying eggs in the pan.

example.target: Imaš li recept za ovaj kolač?
example.gloss: Do you have the recipe for this cake?
What are the ingredients of this soup?
Add a little oil to the pan.

words.note: feminine i-declension: genitive soli; distinctly Croatian (vs 'so')

example.gloss: Add a little salt to the soup.

words.note: fleeting a: genitive papra; the vegetable is paprika

example.gloss: We need salt and pepper.
Do you take your coffee with sugar?

example.target: Za kolač trebamo brašno i jaja.
example.gloss: For the cake we need flour and eggs.
We eat rice with the meat.
For dinner I'm cooking pasta.
We need a kilo of potatoes.
I'm cutting onions and crying.
I don't like garlic.
The rabbit is eating a carrot.

example.target: Salata je od rajčice i luka.
example.gloss: The salad is made of tomato and onion.

example.target: Paprika može biti crvena, žuta ili zelena.
example.gloss: Peppers can be red, yellow, or green.
With lunch we eat green salad.
Sarma is made from cabbage.
In the morning I eat a banana.
Orange juice is healthy.
I put lemon in my tea.
Grapes grow in Dalmatia.
Strawberries are sweet.
Grandma is baking a cake for the birthday.
I bought you a chocolate bar.
In summer we eat ice cream on the beach.

example.target: Stavljam med u čaj kad sam bolestan.
example.gloss: I put honey in my tea when I'm sick.

words.note: fleeting a: genitive maslaca; distinctly Croatian (vs 'puter')

example.gloss: I spread butter on bread.

example.target: Sendvič sa šunkom i sirom, molim.
example.gloss: A ham and cheese sandwich, please.
We grill sausages on the barbecue.

example.target: Piletina s rižom je moj najdraži ručak.
example.gloss: Chicken with rice is my favorite lunch.

example.target: Juha od govedine je jako dobra.
example.gloss: Beef soup is very good.
I don't eat pork.
Dad is cooking lunch today.

words.en: to bake / to roast / to fry

example.gloss: I'm baking bread in the oven.
I'm cutting bread with a knife.
Stir the soup slowly.
I want to order a pizza.
What is your favorite animal?
Our dog is called Rex.
The cat is sleeping on the couch.
Birds are singing in the park.

example.target: Konj je velika i jaka životinja.
example.gloss: The horse is a big and strong animal.
The cow gives us milk.
Pigs live in the countryside.
The hen lays eggs.
Sheep eat grass in the meadow.
Grandma has two goats.
A bear lives in the forest.
The wolf is a dangerous animal.
The fox is cunning.
The rabbit runs fast.
The cat is chasing the mouse.
I'm afraid of snakes.

words.note: fleeting a: genitive kukca; distinctly Croatian (vs 'buba/insekt')

example.target: Ljeti je u vrtu puno kukaca.
example.gloss: In summer there are lots of insects in the garden.
Bees give us honey.

example.target: Leptir leti s cvijeta na cvijet.
example.gloss: The butterfly flies from flower to flower.
On weekends we walk in the forest.
Behind the house is a big field.
Flowers grow in the meadow.
The grass is wet from the rain.

words.note: plural: listovi; lišće = foliage (collective); also a sheet of paper

example.target: U jesen listovi padaju s drveća.
example.gloss: In autumn the leaves fall from the trees.
A bird is sitting on a branch.
The child throws a stone into the water.
The children are playing in the sand.
In the mountains the air is clean.
The soil in the garden is wet.
I love spending time in nature.
We must protect the environment.
We climbed the hill.
At night we look at the stars.

words.en: to stroll / to take a walk

example.gloss: On Sundays we walk along the river.
In the morning I feed the cat.
